title: Teline orientalis
taxonomy: Teline orientalis is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. Teline orientalis is closely related to the genus Cytisus, which shares similar characteristics and growth habits. The species is further classified as a type of shrub or small tree, with a range of growth patterns depending on environmental conditions. Within the Fabaceae family, Teline orientalis is classified in the subfamily Faboideae, a group of legumes that includes many species of shrubs and trees.
uses: Teline orientalis, also known as golden medick or Greek clover, has been traditionally used in various folk remedies. The plant has been employed to treat a range of health issues, including fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The seeds of Teline orientalis are said to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers are reported to have been used to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of Teline orientalis as a medicinal plant.

intro: Teline orientalis, also known as Oriental Star or Turkish Star, is a small, yellow-flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, and its extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, making it beneficial for wound healing, digestive health, and immune system support. Bioactive constituents of Teline orientalis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Teline orientalis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
